rag_rules: Preserve the existing AADL structure and place any added annex content only within the owner scope it is intended for.
Respect owner and scope boundaries: component-level content belongs to component types, while implementation-scoped content belongs to implementations.
Do not alter subcomponents, features, connections, or properties when fusing annex content into the model.
generic_rules: Use the input AADL model as the authority for valid owners and available structural elements.
Insert new content after the owning declaration in a way that keeps surrounding architecture unchanged.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: Implementation-scoped content should remain within the target implementation block.
Keep annex additions syntactically separate from structural declarations so routing and architecture remain intact.
anti_patterns: Do not move content between type and implementation scopes.
Do not rewrite or infer architectural elements while inserting annex content.
irrelevant_topics: Validator-driven expression repairs for booleans, properties, or event ports are not relevant without active diagnostics.
Requirement semantics about signal-routing behavior are not needed for owner/scope fusion decisions.
